name: West Jackson Cdc
description_short: 501(c)(3) nonprofit community development organization serving West Jackson neighborhoods with youth programs, housing initiatives, and economic development focused on areas surrounding Jackson State University.
description_long:
West Jackson Community Development Corporation (West Jackson CDC) is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization established to address systemic urban decline in Jackson, Mississippi. The organization specifically targets Wards Three and Five, which have historically faced challenges including high unemployment, crime, poverty, absentee landlords, and environmental neglect. Operating in partnership with Jackson State University, local government, financial institutions, and community organizations, West Jackson CDC has grown from a three-person operation to a team of thirteen full-time staff members plus contractual and part-time workers.

West Jackson CDC offers a range of community-focused services structured around four strategic pillars: housing development, economic development, youth development, and community/organizational development. Their primary programs include YouthBuild (with instruction, job development, case management, and construction training components), mentoring coordination, and community events like the annual Lynch Street Cultural Arts Festival. The organization is governed by a 13-person Board of Directors comprising community residents, business owners, city representatives, and civic leaders, with Mrs. Linda H. Carter serving as Executive Director.

What distinguishes West Jackson CDC is its collaborative university-based model that bridges Jackson State University, local government, financial institutions, neighborhood associations, churches, and community residents into a unified development network. Rather than offering direct financial services, the organization facilitates systemic community change through partnership building and coordinated programming. The 33-year history of the Lynch Street Cultural Arts Festival demonstrates sustained community engagement and cultural preservation alongside economic development efforts.

West Jackson CDC is fundamentally a community development and youth services organization rather than a consumer finance provider. While their mission includes economic development, they do not offer credit counseling, debt relief, loans, or traditional financial products. Consumers seeking free credit counseling or housing counselor services should verify whether the organization provides these specific services, as their website emphasizes youth programs and neighborhood revitalization over consumer financial assistance.
pros: Established 501(c)(3) nonprofit with transparent governance structure and 13-person Board of Directors
University partnership model with Jackson State University provides institutional stability and resources
Comprehensive YouthBuild program including instruction, job development, case management, and construction training
Long track record of community engagement demonstrated by 33-year-running Lynch Street Cultural Arts Festival
Multi-sector collaborative approach integrating local government, financial institutions, churches, and neighborhood associations
Dedicated mentoring coordinator position indicates commitment to youth guidance beyond job training
Located directly in service area (1635 Raymond Road, Jackson) with established community roots
cons: Website does not explicitly advertise free credit counseling or HUD-approved housing counselor services despite free-help categorization
No information provided about specific financial literacy or consumer finance education programs
Limited details on how community members can directly access services beyond youth programs
No statement of financial transparency, annual reports, or charity ratings (GiveWell, Charity Navigator) linked on website
Geographic limitation to Wards Three and Five may exclude some Jackson residents from primary service area
best_for: Jackson youth (especially ages served by YouthBuild) seeking job training and career development
West Jackson neighborhood residents interested in community organizing and economic revitalization
Small business owners and community leaders collaborating on neighborhood development
Families seeking affordable community events and cultural celebrations
diagnosis: West Jackson CDC is best for Jackson youth seeking job training and neighborhood residents engaged in community revitalization efforts. However, this organization primarily focuses on youth development and community development rather than consumer financial assistance; verify directly whether they offer free credit counseling or housing counselor services before seeking financial guidance.
services: YouthBuild job training and instruction programs
Neighborhood association coordination and leadership development
Community partnership facilitation with government and financial institutions
Annual Lynch Street Cultural Arts Festival organization
Economic development initiatives and business collaboration
Community event coordination with health fair and vendor opportunities
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
